RULING - Matter of Plaintiff/Respondent's peremptory challenge against Judge Goldsmith was argued by counsel. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff/Respondent Darnice Linton's C.C.P. Section 170.6 peremptory challenge against Hon. Ernest H. Goldsmith is denied as untimely. The motion to compel was argued by counsel. Parties entered into a stipulation that the depositions shall be conducted on 1/12/15 and 1/13/15, but that Defendant DeSoto does not waive its statutory objections regarding the depositions. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/RESPONDENT DARNICE LINTON'S Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notice And Document Requests To De Soto Cab Company's Person Most Knowledgeable, and Request for Sanctions is denied. The evidence shows that the witness was not produced to due miscommunication from busy schedules of both counsel. (See Seibert Decl., Exs. C-O.) Defense counsel attempted to comply with her obligations under the Discovery Act by offering dates to produce the witnesses within the week or two after November 13 and 14. (Id., Ex. Q.) This issue could have been resolved through meet and confer efforts. Plaintiff's counsel shall pay Defendant sanctions in the amount of $1,750.00 within 30 days of entry of this Order. (See CCP sec. 2023.030(a).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Heather Callier, CSR #13758, Tel No. (916) 498-9288 (302/EHG)
